title: The One With Mac and C.H.E.E.S.E.

lines.text: With Scenes Taken From Episodes Transcribed by: Mindy Mattingly Phillips, guineapig, Ruth Curran, Josh Hodge, and Me.
Joey and Rachel's apartment, Phoebe is helping Joey rehearse for an audition. Phoebe is overacting her part.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The reactor's gonna blow in three seconds, we're never gonna make it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You've got to get out of here! Save yourself!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! I won't leave you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don't worry about me, I'm a robot! I'm just a machine!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No you're not! Not to me!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (she stops reading from the script) Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I am extremely talented!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, you're great! Okay, let's take it from...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (interrupting) No, I mean I was really acting my ass off.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I thought I was pretty good too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah, you're solid. Yeah, you're just no me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y'know what? I think that's enough for now. Yeah. I don't want to be over rehearsed.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (tremendously overacting) Fine! I'll do it without you! (Joey gives her thumbs up) I don't need you or anybody else! I'm gonna make it on my own! (Joey closes the door to his bedroom.) You'll see!! You'll all see!!
Opening Credits
Joey and Rachel's apartment, Joey is memorizing his lines. Chandler, Rachel, and Phoebe are there as well.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Joey) So uh, what's this thing you're auditioning for?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, it's a new TV show. Yeah. I'm up for the part of Mac Macaveli or "Mac." Yeah, I'm a detective and I solve crimes with the help of my robot partner. He's a, he's a Computerized Humanoid Electronically Enhanced Secret Enforcer or-or "C.H.E.E.S.E."

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So Mac and C.H.E.E.S.E.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's the title! Yeah! Y'know they really lucked out that the initials spell cheese.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That is lucky.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh-Wait so Joey if you get this, you're gonna be like the star of your own TV show! I mean you'll be like the Big Cheese! (To Phoebe) Or the Big Mac-Hey! You love those!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, don't get your hopes up, because probably not gonna happen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now-now, why would you say that Joseph?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I mean come on you guys! My own TV show? I just don't know if I'm good enough.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I am.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, what are you talking about? You're a terrific actor.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You really think so?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh, how can you even ask that question?!

[And with that we go into the save the budget portion of the show, which features flashbacks from previous episodes. The first set of auditions feature high lights or low lights of Joey's acting career. The first flashback is from The One With The Lesbian Wedding.]
Monica and Rachel's, everyone is there to watch Joey's first scene as Dr. Drake Remoray.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Whoa, she's pretty. (Mentioning the girl on TV)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, and oh she's really nice too. She taught me all about how to work the cameras, and smell-the-fart acting.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry, what?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's like, you got so many lines to learn so fast, that sometimes you need a minute to remember your next one. So while you're thinkin' of it, you take this big pause where you look all intense, you know, like this. (Does this intense look where it looks like he's smelling a fart.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, ok.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (there's a gunshot on TV) There's my scene, there's my scene. [Joey on tv] "Mrs. Wallace, I'm Dr. Drake Ramoray, your sister's neurosurgeon.  I'm afraid the situation is much worse than we expected. Your sister is suffering from a..." (Does the smell-the-fart look.)
[The next flashback is from The One With The Butt.  The gang is watching Joey in Freud!]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on stage in an Austrian accent) Vell, Eva, ve've done some excellent vork here, and I vould have to say, your pwoblem is qviiite clear. (He goes into a song and dance number.)
All you want is a dingle,
What you envy's a schwang,
A thing through which you can tinkle,
Or play with, or simply let hang...
[The next flashback is also from The One With The Butt and it's also on the soundtrack.  He's Joey telling everyone about his big break in Monica and Rachel's apartment.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I play Al Pacino's butt. All right? He goes into the shower, and then- I'm his butt.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (trying not to laugh) Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: C'mon, you guys. This is a real movie, and Al Pacino's in it, and that's big!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no, it's terrific, it's- it's- y'know, you deserve this, after all your years of struggling, you've finally been able to crack your way into show business.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, okay, fine! Make jokes, I don't care! This is a big break for me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're right, you're right, it is.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So you gonna invite us all to the big opening?
[Cut back to Joey about to leave for his audition for Mac and C.H.E.E.S.E.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right well, I'm outta here. Wish me luck.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (overacting with a song this time) (singing) Gooood luck! Gooood luck! We all wish you good luuuuuuuuck!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, whatever. (Exits.)
Central Perk, Chandler and Ross are playing chess and are both studying the board intently.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's your turn.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, are we playing this?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering, dejectedly) Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! How'd the audition go?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Terrible! I messed up every line! I shouldn't even be an actor!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait a minute, are you doing that thing where you pretend it didn't go well but it really did go well?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, did I fool ya?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Totally!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So it did go well.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, it went amazingly well!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh that's great!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, it's down to me and two other guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And I know both of them, they're really good. One of them is the guy from those allergy commercials who's always getting chased by those big flowers...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I love that guy! (Laughs.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh-oh, what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (stops laughing) What am I doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm just so nervous! Y'know? The callback isn't until tomorrow at five. I feel like my head is going to explode!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it is overdue.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, don't worry. Okay? You're gonna be fine.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There's just so much pressure. I mean no offense, but what you guys do is very different. I don't know if you'd understand.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, none of us have to deal with pressure at our jobs.
[Thus starts another series of flashbacks all dealing with the pressure the rest of the Friends have to deal with in their jobs. The first flashback is from The One With The Stoned Guy.]
Chandler's office. He's on the phone, agitated.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (on phone) Yes, Fran. I know what time it is, but I'm looking at the WENUS and I'm not happy!
[The next flashback is from The One With The Prom Video.  Monica is at a job interview at a new restaurant.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright, well I'm tearing the lettuce.

lines.speaker: Interviewer
lines.text: Uh-huh. Is it dirty?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh-oh, no no don't worry, I'm gonna wash it.

lines.speaker: Interviewer
lines.text: Don't, I like it dirty.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's your call.

lines.speaker: Interviewer
lines.text: So, uh, what are you going to do next?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I thought that I would cut up the tomatos.

lines.speaker: Interviewer
lines.text: Are they, uh, firm?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They'r alright.

lines.speaker: Interviewer
lines.text: You sure they haven't gone bad? You're sure they're not very, very bad?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No really, they're OK.

lines.speaker: Interviewer
lines.text: You gonna slice them up real nice?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Actually, I was gonna do them jullienne.

lines.speaker: Interviewer
lines.text: Aaaahhhhhhh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm outa here. [Monica leaves]
[The next flashback is from The One With Rachel's Crush.]
Bloomingdale's, Rachel's new job.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (on the phone) Monica, I'm quitting! I just helped an 81 year old woman put on a thong and she didn't even buy it! (Pause) I'm telling you I'm quitting! That's it! I'm talking to my boss right now! (Pause) Yes I am! (Pause) Yes I am! Yes I am! Yes I am! Yes I am! Yes I am! Okay bye, call me when you get this message. (Hangs up.)
[The next flashback is from The One With The Dozen Lasagnas, Phoebe is telling everyone but Rachel about the pass Paulo made on her.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, what happened?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, he came in for a massage and everything was fine until... (The flashback shows Paulo lying face down on the massage table and slowly moving his hands up Phoebe's legs and grabbing her butt.)

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Ohhhhhh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And all of the sudden his hands weren't the problem anymore. (The flashback shows Paulo rolling over and showing Phoebe his equipment.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Was it...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, Boy Scouts could've camped under it!
[The next flashback is from The One With Ross's Sandwich, Ross his confronting his boss about him eating Ross's sandwich.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You ate my sandwich?

lines.speaker: Dr. Leedbetter
lines.text: It was a simple mistake. It could happen to anyone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (getting upset) Oh-oh really?

lines.speaker: Dr. Leedbetter
lines.text: Now-now calm down. Come look in my office, some of it my still be in the trash.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (jumping to his feet in anger) What?

lines.speaker: Dr. Leedbetter
lines.text: Well, it was quite large. I-I-I-I-I had to throw most of it away.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You-you-you-you (trying to remain in control) threw my sandwich away!
[Cut to an outside shot of the museum.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (losing control, we hear him shout outside) MY SANDWICH?!!!
[Cut to a shot of a park.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: MY SANDWICH!!!!!! (Ross's scream scares a flight of pigeons away.)
[Cut back to Central Perk.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I want this part so much! Y'know? If I don't get this part I'm never gonna eat Macaroni and Cheese again!-No, I didn't say that! That's a lie.
Joey and Rachel's apartment, Chandler is there talking to the chick and duck.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh come on guys, it's not like I moved to Europe! I just moved across the hall! And we would have you over all the time if it weren't for (struggles to get this out) Monica's allergies. (The duck quacks.) You're right, I could never lie to you. She hates you. (The phone rings.) Should I get that? (Laughs, then answers the phone.) Hello? (Listens) Uh no, Joey's not here right now. Can I take a message? (Listens) Yeah, okay so the audition has been moved from 5:00 to 2:30? (Listens) Okay great. (Listens.) Bye. (Hangs up the phone and goes to write the message on the Magna-Doodle on the door but Monica walks in and forces him to jump out of the way.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (startled) Ahh! Aren't you dressed yet?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (looks down at his clothes) Am I naked again?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're supposed to meet my parents in 15 minutes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah okay, I was just talking to the guys. Just look at them I mean, is it okay if they come visit?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wh?! What about my allergies?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh right, your allergies. (Monica leaves and to the chick and duck) All her, she hates you. (Chandler leaves without finishing the message for Joey.)
Joey and Rachel's apartment, Joey and Chandler are playing foosball and Joey scores a goal.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes!! Ha-ha!! All right! Hey! How cool would it be if you could watch like a real life-sized version of this? Huh? I mean how crazy would that be?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: As crazy as soccer?
The phone rings and Joey answers it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on phone) Hello? (Listens) What are you talking about? The audition's not 'til 5:00! (Chandler suddenly remembers and looks at the unfinished message then tries to sneak over and finish it as Joey listens.) Well, nobody told me! (Listens) Who'd you talk to? (Listens and turns around to see Chandler trying to finish the message.) Nevermind! (Hangs up.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mean you didn't get it from this?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The allergy guy got the part! Thanks!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, maybe we can fix it y'know? Maybe we can send him some-some big-big flowers and scare him!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How could you do this to me Chandler?! This part could've turned my whole career around!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I messed up. Okay? I'm sorry, I really messed up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, you don't even live here anymore! What are you doing answering my phone? I have my machine!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Which I bought for ya. Taught ya how to use it. You thought it was a copier. Look, if there was anything I could do, I would do it. Okay? But everybody's allowed one mistake, right?

[Joey just laughs as a third set of flashbacks featuring Chandler's mistakes starts. The first flashback is from The One With The Prom Video.  It's Chandler telling Phoebe how much he hates the bracelet Joey bought him.  They're both at Central Perk.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know he coulda gotten me a VCR, he coulda gotten me a set of golf clubs, but no, he has to get me the woman repeller!  The eyesore from the Liberace house of crap!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's not that bad.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, yeah, easy for you to say, you don't have to walk around sporting some reject from the Mr. T collection. [Joey walks in behind Chandler]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Chandler, Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I pity the fool who puts on my jewelry, I do, I do. I pity the fool that. . . [turns around and sees Joey] Hi!
[The next flashback is from The One Where Chandler Crosses The Line.  He's telling Joey that he kissed Cathy.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I mean when you were late last night, Kathy and I got to talking, and one thing to another and...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And what?! Did you sleep with her?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! No! No! I just kissed her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?!! That's even worse!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How is that worse?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know! But it's the same!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're right, I have no excuses! I was totally over the line.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Over the line?! You-you're-you're so far past the line, that you-you can't even see the line! The line is a dot to you!
[Cut back to Joey and Rachel's apartment.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I'm not saying that you should magically forgive me! But you're not perfect! You've made some errors in judgment too!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Name one!

[Chandler proceeds to point out Joey's errors in this forth set of flashbacks. The first flashback is from The One With The Cat.   Chandler has just returned home to see that their apartment has been cleaned out and finds Joey trapped in the entertainment center.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (lets him out) What happened?!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (getting out) Awww, man! He promised he wouldn't take the chairs!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What the hell happened?!! How were you locked in?!! And where the hell is all of our stuff?!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, this guy came by to look at the unit and-and he said he didn't think big enough to fit a grown man!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So--You got in voluntarily?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I was tryin' to make a sale!! Oh, man, if I ever run into that guy again, do you know what I'm gonna do?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: BEND OVER?!!!
[The next flashback is from The One With The Candy Hearts.  Joey and Chandler are waiting at a restaurant as Lorraine and her friend arrive.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok, now, remember, no trading. You get the pretty one, I get the mess.

lines.speaker: Lorraine
lines.text: Hi, Joey. (Sees Chandler.) Well well, look what you brought.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: ...And what did you bring?

lines.speaker: Lorraine
lines.text: She's checking the coats. Joey, I'm gonna go wash the cab smell off my hands. Will you get me a white Zinfandel, and a glass of red for Janice.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Janice?
Lorraine leaves. Joey shakes his head as though to say, 'It can't be the same Janice.' Janice enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Janice?!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh.... my.... God.
[The next flashback is from The One With Ross's New Girlfriend.  Chandler is telling Ross and Joey that Joey's tailor took advantage of him.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey's tailor...took advantage of me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No way!  I've been going to the guy for 12 years.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh come on!  He said he was going to do my inseam, and he ran his hand up my leg, and then, there was definite...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Cupping.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's how they do pants!  Ross, will you tell him? Isn't that how they measure pants?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, yes it is. In prison!
[Cut back to the present.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I said name one!
Commercial Break
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica and Rachel are consoling Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't believe I did this! What an idiot!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, stop beating yourself up! People make mistakes! These things happen! There aren't any message you've forgotten to give me are there? (Chandler has a disgusted look on his face.) Apparently you're not very good at it! I...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you think he'll ever forgive me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Of course he will! But Chandler the most important thing is you forgive yourself!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what? I-I kinda have.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Already? That's pretty bad what you did.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what? He will forgive you. And I like to bring a pad with me when I go answer the phone just in case... (Chandler gets that disgusted look back.) Okay...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You didn't see how mad he was, y'know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sure he will forgive you. Look, we have all been there! Y'know, you fight, you make up, it's just the way it works.
[We then go into another set of flashbacks of famous fights. The first is the second breakup of Ross and Rachel from The One With The Jellyfish.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It took two people to break up this relationship!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! You and that girl from that copy place, which yesterday you took full responsibility for!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I didn't know what I was taking full responsibility for! Okay?! I didn't finish the whole letter!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I fell asleep!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (mocking him) You fell asleep?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It was 5:30 in the morning, and you had rambled on for 18 pages. Front and back!! (they go into the living room, trapping Monica, Chandler, and Joey in the kitchen) (to Rachel) Oh-oh-oh, and by the way, Y-O-U-apostrophe-R-E means 'you are,' Y-O-U-R means 'your!'

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know I can't believe I even thought about getting back together again! We are so over!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (starts to cry) FINE BY ME!! (he opens the door and traps Chandler behind it)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And hey! Just so you know, it's not that common! It doesn't happen to every guy! And it is a big deal!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (coming out from behind the door) I KNEW IT!!!!
[The next flashback is from The One After The Superbowl, Part II.  Monica and Rachel are fighting over who gets to see Jean-Claude Van Damme.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You had no right to go out with him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That is the most ridiculous...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You sold me out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I did not sell you out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes you did.  You absolutely sold me...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Would you let me talk. [flicks Monica on the forehead]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did you just flick me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK, well, you wouldn't let me finish and I was jus- [Monica flicks her back] Ow. That hurt [flicks Monica]
They keep flicking each other. This turns into slapping each other.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!  All right.  Now, let's not do this!
Rachel tackles Monica onto the couch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Happy thoughts!  Happy thoughts!

They wrestle on the couch for a little while until Monica gets the upper hand and pulls Rachel off of the couch by her sock.  Monica removes Rachel's sock and starts beating her with it.)This leads to wrestling on the floor. This finally angers Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK, now I'm gonna kick some ass.
Phoebe grabs each of them by an ear.

lines.speaker: Monica and Rachel
lines.text: Ow! Ow! Ow!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know!  I know!  I know!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Alright, now I will let go if you both stop.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Fine!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fine!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There we go.You know what, if we were in prison, you guys would be, like, my bitches.
[The next flashback is from The One Where No One's Ready.  Joey is retaliating against Chandler hiding his underwear by wearing a whole bunch of clothes.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, buddy-boy. Here it is. You hide my clothes, I'm wearing everything you own.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look at me! I'm Chandler! Could I be wearing any more clothes? Maybe if I wasn't going commando...
Chandler does this throat sound.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Whew, it's hot with all of this stuff on. I ah, I better not do any, I don't know, lunges. (starts doing lunges)

[The next flashback is from The One Where Eddie Moves In.   It's the sequence where Joey and Chandler are both saddened that they're not living together anymore. All By Myself is playing in the background.]
[Joey's place. He goes to say something to Chandler in the other chair but no one's there. He goes to call Chandler but decides not to and throws the phone back down.]
[Chandler's. He's playing foosball by himself.]
[Joey's. Playing ping pong by himself.]
[Chandler is sitting in front of a window while it's raining outside. We see Joey through a rainy window. The camera zooms out to show it's just his tabletop water sculpture.]
Joey and Rachel's apartment, Chandler is entering with a peace offering of a Joey Special, two pizzas.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey? Got you a Joey Special, two pizzas! Joe? (The phone rings and he answers it) (On phone) Hello? (Takes the phone away from his mouth when he realizes what he just did and yells.) Damnit! (Back on phone.) Hello? (Listens.) No, Joey's not here right now, but I can take a message I think. (Listens) He's still got a chance for the part?! Oh, that's great news! (Listens) Well no obviously not for the actor who was mauled by his dog. (Listens) Oh well, that's great. I will give Joey the message. Thank you! (Hangs up and goes to write the message on the Magna-Doodle.) Yes! (Reading what he's writing) Okay, Mac audition at 2:00. Allergy actor attacked. (Pause) By dog not flowers.
Joey and Rachel's apartment, Joey is entering to find Chandler waiting patiently for him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Please tell me you got the message!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What message?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The actor playing Mac couldn't do it, they needed to see you at 2 o'clock.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?! It's 6 o'clock!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Du-du-I wrote it, I wrote it on the board! I wrote it on the board, then I went all over New York City looking for ya! I went to Ross's! I went to the coffeehouse! I went to any place that they made sandwiches!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can't believe this Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sorry! I-I-I don't know what to say.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well you-you-you-you might say congratulations! I saw the board! I went to the audition! I got the part!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (angrily) Is that supposed to be funny! I was really worried over here!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, well I'm uh...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, sometimes that fake out thing is just mean!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh wow! Okay man, I'm sorry. I did not mean to make you feel bad.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well that's good. Because you didn't! And I'm incredibly happy for ya!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (shocked) That's mean! You really had me going there!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, we could do this all day.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, you're right. Okay look, listen, let's talk about what a huge star I'm gonna be!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You are gonna be a huge star! I'm gonna hug ya!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You hug me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right!

[What follows is the final set of flashbacks that feature a whole lot of lovin' between Chandler and Joey. There's no words, just hugs and even the kiss from The One With The Monkey. All of this is set to the song, You've Really Got a Hold on Me!]
[Cut back to the present, they are still in each other's embrace.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, do we do this too much?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think so. Yeah, get off me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.
They separate.
Ending Credits
Joey and Rachel's apartment, Joey and Phoebe are reading a scene from Mac and C.H.E.E.S.E.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on C.H.E.E.S.E., I'm not leaving without you! Try routing your backup source through your primary CPU.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (overacting badly) I can't! My circuits are fried! They're fried I tell you!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! (Back to reading the scene.) Well then I'll just have to carry you.
Suddenly Ross jumps up from behind the counter armed with a plastic gun. By the way, he's overacting too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That'll be a neat trick, when you're, (looks at the script) when you're dead!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross, you don't have to yell.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: There was just an explosion, okay? My hearing would be impaired.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I thought you were excellent! In fact for a minute there I was like, "Ooh, where'd Ross go?"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you! And I have to say that first scene when you meet Mac...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God. I mean...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y'know what? I think that's enough for today. Thanks for your help! (He grabs their scripts and heads for his room.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Ross) He's holding us back.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Totally.
End
